description: Phase 2: Make the subagent architecture actually functional. Fix --agents CLI integration, path resolution, documentation accuracy, and hook-loop parity.

userStories.title: Update ralph.sh to register custom agents via --agents flag
userStories.description: As a SuperRalph user, I want ralph.sh to register implementer, spec-reviewer, and code-quality-reviewer as custom agents so that the orchestrator can spawn them via the Agent tool.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph.sh reads agent prompt files from $PLUGIN_DIR/agents/ directory (implementer-prompt.md, spec-reviewer-prompt.md, code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)
ralph.sh builds a JSON object for the --agents flag with three agents: implementer, spec-reviewer, code-quality-reviewer
Each agent definition includes description and prompt fields populated from the agent prompt files
The claude command in the main loop includes --agents with the built JSON
Agent prompt content is properly escaped for JSON embedding (newlines, quotes)
ralph.sh passes shellcheck with no errors
ralph.sh passes bash -n syntax check
userStories.notes: Use jq to build the JSON safely (handles escaping). The --agents flag format is: '{"name": {"description": "...", "prompt": "..."}}'.

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md.template to use subagent_type instead of file paths
userStories.description: As a SuperRalph orchestrator, I want the template to reference agents by subagent_type name so that the Agent tool can dispatch them without needing file paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace 'Read agents/implementer-prompt.md from the plugin directory' with 'Use the Agent tool with subagent_type=implementer'
Replace 'Read agents/spec-reviewer-prompt.md from the plugin directory' with 'Use the Agent tool with subagent_type=spec-reviewer'
Replace 'Read agents/code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md from the plugin directory' with 'Use the Agent tool with subagent_type=code-quality-reviewer'
No file path references to agents/ directory remain in the template
All discipline placeholders still work ({TDD_DISCIPLINE} etc.)
The <promise>COMPLETE</promise> stop signal is preserved
userStories.notes: The --agents flag registers these agent types at the CLI level, so the orchestrator just references them by name.

userStories.title: Fix sub-technique discipline references to use inline content
userStories.description: As a SuperRalph iteration agent, I need sub-technique content available inline so that references like 'See disciplines/root-cause-tracing.md' actually work.
acceptanceCriteria: Add new placeholders to CLAUDE.md.template: {ROOT_CAUSE_TRACING}, {DEFENSE_IN_DEPTH}, {TESTING_ANTI_PATTERNS}
Update ralph.sh build_instructions() to replace these placeholders with file contents from disciplines/
Update debugging.md references from 'See disciplines/root-cause-tracing.md' to 'See the Root Cause Tracing section below' (since content will be inline)
Update debugging.md references from 'See disciplines/defense-in-depth.md' to 'See the Defense in Depth section below'
Update tdd.md references from 'See disciplines/testing-anti-patterns.md' to 'See the Testing Anti-Patterns section below'
ralph.sh passes shellcheck with no errors
userStories.notes: Same pattern as existing discipline injection. Add sub-sections under the parent discipline sections.

userStories.title: Update stop-hook.sh to include subagent instructions in prompt
userStories.description: As a hook-loop user, I want the continuation prompt to instruct the agent to use subagents so that hook-loop has the same subagent behavior as bash-loop.
acceptanceCriteria: stop-hook.sh prompt includes instructions to use Agent tool with subagent_type for implementer, spec-reviewer, and code-quality-reviewer
Instructions match the subagent pipeline from CLAUDE.md.template (implementer -> spec review -> code quality review)
Include fix-and-review cycle instructions (max 2 cycles)
stop-hook.sh passes shellcheck with no errors
stop-hook.sh passes bash -n syntax check
userStories.notes: The hook-loop can't register --agents at the CLI level since it runs within an existing session. But the existing session should already have Agent tool available. The prompt just needs to instruct usage.

userStories.title: Fix README.md accuracy issues
userStories.description: As a potential user, I want the README to accurately describe dependencies and credits so that I can trust the documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove or update 'No dependencies' claim to mention jq and perl as requirements
Fix Superpowers credit URL to point to the correct repository (github.com/obra/superpowers, not superpower-chatgpt)
Add a Prerequisites section listing: claude CLI, jq, perl
Note that banner.jpg is a placeholder in the assets/ directory
Remove or clarify 'dev-browser skill' references — note it requires a browser automation tool
userStories.notes: The README currently claims zero dependencies but the scripts require jq and perl.

userStories.title: Update DEVELOPMENT.md for v2.0 changes
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want DEVELOPMENT.md to reflect the v2.0 architecture so that the handoff document is accurate.
acceptanceCriteria: Update file structure diagram to include agents/ directory, sub-technique files, find-polluter.sh, docs/plans/
Update discipline count from 5 to 8 (add root-cause-tracing, defense-in-depth, testing-anti-patterns)
Add section describing the subagent architecture (--agents flag, three agent types)
Update version references from 1.0.0 to 2.0.0
Add the --agents CLI integration to the bash-loop architecture description
userStories.notes: DEVELOPMENT.md is the handoff document for future maintainers.
